concept: Soap / Cleansing Material Used with Water
description: A cleansing material used with water to lift oil and dirt from bodies, cloth, or objects. It may be hard, soft, or liquid according to its making; 'sawa' (wash) is the action, while 'sawathi' is the material added to the water and rubbing.
sound_symbolism: Fine 'sa' hisses into broad 'a'. Stressed 'wa' rounds the lips and opens fully, so 'sawa' (wash) is heard intact at the opening. Final 'thi' brings a thin stream of breath to the teeth and lifts into bright 'i'. Two open vowels flow toward a clean narrow finish.
grammatical_notes: A mass or countable noun according to form: 'mia roe sawathi lo soeli sawa.' (I wash the cups with soap.) A surrounding clause can identify its ingredients and intended use. Fragrance, safety, and ecological effect depend on the actual soap and conditions.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Soap invites questions about ingredients, water, packaging, and what leaves through the drain after the washing is done.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fat, oil, and alkaline ash became household soap through practiced ratios and patient curing.

semantic_domains.physical: material that loosens oil and dirt in water
